notes: +1 universally. Confirmed: paradox mirage=3 (mirage 2+1), paradox whirl=4 (whirl 3+1), paradox torque=5 (torque 4+1).

Spinning contributes +1 ADD as a body-spin modifier. The bonus is the same on rotational and non-rotational bases. On rotational bases (whirl, mirage, torque, blender) the base trick's stall component is already counted in the base ADD value, so the spinning bonus does not stack further. Worked examples: spinning clipper = 3, spinning butterfly = 4, spinning whirl = 4, spinning torque = 5.
Inspinning contributes +1 ADD: any spin is +1. Forward-rotation spin, distinct in direction from spinning's default back-rotation; same ADD weight as spinning.
+1 universally. Confirmed: ducking clipper=3, ducking butterfly=4, ducking whirl=4, ducking osis=4.
+1 universally. Confirmed: symposium whirl=4, symposium dlo=4, symposium torque=5 (torque 4+1).
+1 universally. Confirmed: gyro torque=mobius=5 (torque 4+1), gyro whirl=4 (whirl 3+1), gyro butterfly=4 (butterfly 3+1).
+1 universally; a set / uptime foot relocation (not a body movement), so classified as a set modifier. Confirmed: stepping whirl=4, stepping osis=4, stepping butterfly=4, stepping drifter=4.
+1 universally. Confirmed: tapping whirl=4, tapping clipper=3 (clipper 2+1).
+1 universally. Confirmed: blazing legover=3 (legover 2+1).

Barraging contributes +2 ADD as a two-dex set. Structurally count-bearing: two dex steps inside the set. Worked examples: barraging + osis = baroque = 5 (osis 3 + 2); barraging + legover = flurry = 4 (legover 2 + 2). Classified as a set modifier to match the two-dex set semantics.
+1 universally. Confirmed: weaving clipper=3, weaving butterfly=4.
+1 universally. Confirmed: diving butterfly=4 (butterfly 3+1).
Swirling contributes +1 ADD as a body-spin modifier, parallel to spinning. The bonus is the same on rotational and non-rotational bases.
+1 universally. Confirmed: whirling osis=blender=4 (osis 3+1).

Blurry contributes +1 ADD as a set modifier. The bonus is the same on rotational and non-rotational bases. Several named blurry compounds carry deeper structural decompositions where blurry stands for a multi-modifier expansion: Blur = Stepping Paradox Mirage, Ripwalk = Stepping Butterfly. In those cases the named compound's ADD value derives from the expanded decomposition rather than from a flat blurry + base addition. Worked examples under +1: blurry clipper = 3, blurry butterfly = 4, blurry mirage = 3.

Atomic = +1 ADD on every base; it has no rotational class (the +2-rotational reading is retired). Any count beyond +1 comes only from a separate conditional X-Dex (+1), which fires solely on a far move after a non-paradox dex on an eligible receiver (mirage, illusion, whirl, torque, drifter) and is recorded only where the notation carries [XDEX]. Worked examples: atomic butterfly = legbeater = 4 (butterfly 3 + 1, butterfly is a non-receiver, no X-Dex); atom smasher = atomic far mirage = 4 (mirage 2 + 1 + far-form X-Dex 1); silo = atomic torque = 5 (near form, no X-Dex). Atomic is the modern uptime outward-dex set; illusioning is a distinct downtime move, not an equivalent name for atomic.
+1 universally. Confirmed: pixie mirage=smear=3 (mirage 2+1), pixie butterfly=dimwalk=4 (butterfly 3+1), pixie osis=4 (osis 3+1).
+1 universally. Confirmed: fairy mirage=3 (mirage 2+1), fairy butterfly=4 (butterfly 3+1).

Quantum contributes +1 ADD universally as a set modifier. Worked examples: quantum mirage = 3 (mirage 2 + 1), quantum butterfly = 4 (butterfly 3 + 1). The named set Sailing decomposes as Pixie + Quantum (1 + 1 = 2 ADD).

Nuclear contributes +2 ADDs as a set modifier. Structurally nuclear = paradox dex (+1) + downtime illusioning dex (+1) = +2 (distinct from atomic, whose second dex is an uptime set; nuclear itself carries no X-Dex). Named exception: Sumo (Nuclear Mirage = 5 ADD) gains its 5th ADD from an X-Dex on the following mirage.

X-Dex contributes +1 ADD per instance. Worked example: Atom Smasher = 2 dex + 1 del + 1 X-Dex = 4 ADD. The Freestyle Concepts terminology describes X-Dex as representing the added difficulty of dexterities involving complete circling of the footbag, similar to symposium and paradox. Whether X-Dex acts as a universal prefix modifier or only fires in specific constructions is an open ontology question; current usage is construction-specific.

Furious is a two-dex uptime set contributing +2 ADD. Historically grouped with barraging; current doctrine holds them distinct by timing, so equivalence is not inferred from the name. Worked example: fury = furious + paradox + mirage = 2 + 1 + 2 = 5 ADD.

Pogo modifier contributes +1: every active pogo compound scores its base +1, corroborated by multiple outside sources and an extra scored dex in the operational notation. The standalone pogo set trick stays 0 ADD.

Shooting is a set primitive that doubles as a modifier. It contributes +3 ADD universally on both rotational and non-rotational bases. Outlier magnitude — most modifiers are +1 or +2. Worked example: Shooting Butterfly = 3 + 3 = 6 ADD.
Rooted is a unique set primitive with one initial dexterity attached. It contributes no ADD value, similar to pogo. Non-scoring 0-ADD modifier.

Railing is a composite set (rooted plus sailing) contributing +2 ADD universally; rooted is a 0-ADD antisymposium discipline and sailing is a +2 dex set. Worked example: Railing Symposium Mirage = dorshanatrix = 5 (mirage 2 + railing 2 + symposium 1).
Surfing is a composite set (fairy plus symposium plus swirling) contributing +3 ADD universally. Worked example: Surfing Blender = big-papa-smurf = 7 (blender 4 + surfing 3).

Splicing is a composite set (gyro plus reving) contributing +2 ADD universally; as an operational chain the two set events read (back) SPIN [BOD] then a reverse-direction OP OUT [DEX], ahead of the unchanged base body. Worked example: Splicing Blender = liquifier = 6 (blender 4 + splicing 2).
Floating is a composite set (quantum plus symposium plus quantum) contributing +3 ADD universally. Worked example: Floating Butterfly = floatation = 6 (butterfly 3 + floating 3).
Warping is a two-dex set (second dex is symposium) contributing +3 ADD universally. Worked example: Warping Mirage = warp = 5 (mirage 2 + warping 3).
Terraging contributes +3 ADD as a body modifier. Distinct from barraging — not a parallel form.

Backside contributes +1 ADD as a body modifier, equivalent to a Symposium-strength bonus. It is a real modifier with its own ADD value, distinct from naming or directional terms like 'inside'. Worked example: the named compound Backside Blur decomposes structurally as Stepping Paradox Symposium Mirage. That expansion is the structural decomposition of the named compound; backside itself contributes +1 ADD and is not additively replaced by the expansion.

Sailing is a set contributing +2 ADD universally (Sailing = pixie + quantum = 2; equivalently a +2 dex set, per the rooted + sailing = railing decomposition). Registers the operator the six Sailing-* compounds already reference via modifier_links. Worked examples: sailing whirl = 5 (whirl 3 + 2), sailing mirage = 4 (mirage 2 + 2).

+1 universally; sibling ducking set to weaving (the bag travels across the body / under the chin before the duck). ADD inherited by ruling from the matching ducking compound, mirroring weaving: zulu clipper=3, zulu butterfly=4.
